merger_name: Redwood Services Group – All IT Australia

acquirers.name: REDWOOD SERVICES GROUP AUSTRALIA PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Computer System Design and Related Services

merger_description:
Redwood Services Group Australia Pty Ltd (**Redwood**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in All IT Australia Pty Ltd (**All IT Australia**) from its existing shareholders Oblivious Pty Ltd, Calatt Pty Ltd, Spurbuck Investments Pty Ltd, and R & D Briggs Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

Redwood’s subsidiaries supply managed IT services, including the provision of software and hardware, to small-to-medium businesses and enterprises across Australia. Redwood is part of Evergreen Services Group (**Evergreen**), which acquires managed IT service providers (**MSPs**) and other technology services companies globally. Evergreen is a portfolio company of Alpine Investors, a private equity firm headquartered in San Francisco, California.

All IT Australia supplies managed IT services to small-to-medium businesses and enterprises.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Redwood Services Group Australia Pty Ltd (Redwood) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the share capital in All IT Australia Pty Ltd (All IT Australia), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Redwood, through its subsidiaries, supplies managed
IT services nationally to small-to-medium businesses and
enterprises, including IT monitoring, system maintenance, IT
helpdesk support, cybersecurity services and data storage, backup
and disaster recovery services, cloud services resale and hardware
resale.
The target, All IT Australia supplies managed IT services, including IT
systems management, monitoring and support, information and
cybersecurity, data backup and disaster recovery, and cloud resale
to small-to-medium business and enterprises predominantly located
in New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland.
The parties overlap in the supply of managed IT services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. The parties’ combined share in the supply of managed IT
services to customers in Australia is low and the increase in
share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small.
b. There are numerous alternative suppliers of managed IT
services to customers in Australia.
The ACCC also had regard to Redwood’s separate notification
waiver application in relation to its proposed acquisition of Newtrend
I.T. Specialists Pty Ltd (WA-60024), which the ACCC assessed
concurrently.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
